notes: kiro-cli has NO native autonomous self-prompting loop (no /goal, no full-auto, no autonomous_mode). The loop is INTERNAL to a single user turn: one seeded multi-step prompt drives the agent's OWN sequential tool cycles (AM(toolUse) -> ToolResults -> ... -> AM(text-only)) with no user prompts between iterations. Because every mid-turn AssistantMessage carries toolUse blocks (parsed as 'assistant', keeps working) and every ToolResults is 'tool_result' (keeps working), the daemon sees ONE continuous working span across all internal iterations — ready ONLY at the final text-only AssistantMessage (turn_done). This is the STRICTEST match of the three siblings for the spec's 'stays working throughout, no ready between iterations' bullet: claudecode flickers per Stop-hook and codex cycles per externally-fed turn, but kiro-cli does neither. No waiting state arises under --trust-all-tools (tools auto-approved). kiro-cli's JSONL has no model/token/cost (sidecar-only), so observations assert agent identity but NOT model/cost/tokens.

text: Session appears in ready (kiro mints its UUID and writes the .jsonl lazily after launch). Anchored UNPINNED to start so a transient proc-PID presession row can't steal the birth.
Spec: 'ready -> working at loop start' — the one seeded multi-step prompt drives the agent into its autonomous internal loop

Spec: state stays working for the ENTIRE autonomous run with NO ready between internal iterations, then 'working -> ready when the goal is reached'. The session holds a single continuous working span across >= 2 internal tool cycles (assistant + tool_result events) and flips to ready exactly once, at the final text-only AssistantMessage. The only strict invariant across the loop is that the session never enters waiting (--trust-all-tools auto-approves; no permission gates). Final state: ready.
